Notes: Initial pseudostate enters Off.
Off state is explicitly present.
Off transitions to On on switchOn which is equivalent.
On is modeled as a composite state.
On transitions to Off on switchOff which is equivalent.
On initially enters LoggedOut which is the unauthenticated idle condition.
The unauthenticated idle state is represented as LoggedOut.
Unauthorized tapCard stays in LoggedOut and shows an error.
Unauthorized guard is represented as !authorized.
Authorized tapCard moves from LoggedOut into the logged in area.
Authorized guard is present on the login transition.
No action variable is used but logged in Idle represents no selected option.
Ready behavior is split across LoggedIn Idle PrintReady and ScanReady.
Logoff is allowed from nonbusy logged in states to LoggedOut.
Starting scan without a detected document stays in ScanReady and shows an error.
Being in ScanReady plus !documentDetected captures this guard.
Starting print with an empty queue stays in PrintReady and shows an error.
Being in PrintReady plus queueEmpty captures this guard.
Choosing scan moves the logged in user to ScanReady.
Entering ScanReady represents selecting scan.
Choosing print moves the logged in user to PrintReady.
Entering PrintReady represents selecting print.
ScanReady starts Scanning when a document is detected and later sends email.
Being in ScanReady plus documentDetected captures this guard.
Busy behavior is modeled by separate Printing and Scanning states rather than one wrapper.
Stop from Printing or Scanning returns to the corresponding ready state.
Done from Printing or Scanning returns to the corresponding ready state.
Paper jam suspends both Printing and Scanning.
Resume memory is represented by separate suspended states returning to the prior task.
Printing state represents the print task.
PrintReady starts Printing when the queue is not empty.
Being in PrintReady plus queueNotEmpty captures this guard.
Out of paper during Printing moves to a paper suspension state.
Scanning with sendToEmail on completion represents scan and email.
Suspension is represented by separate jam and paper suspension states.
Each suspended state resumes the corresponding Printing or Scanning task.
Cancel from each suspended state returns to the appropriate ready state.
Extra ready and suspension states are reasonable refinements.
Extra choice and error transitions are consistent with the described behavior.
No fundamentally incorrect extra guards are present.
Error display and email actions are appropriate details.
LoggedIn composite is a reasonable organization.
No extra regions are present.
No extra history states are present.
